definition:
1. flexible, pliant : στρεπτὸς χιτών a shirt of chain-armour , Lat. lorica annulata, (Iliad by Homer); στρεπταὶ λύγοι pliant  withs, (Euripides): twined, wreathed , [variant datesAnthology Palantina 
2. στρεπτός, οῦ, a collar of twisted  or linked metal , Lat. torques, (Herdotus Historicus), etc. 
3. of pastry, a twist  or roll , (Demosthenes Orator) 
4. metaphorically to be bent  or turned , στρεπτοὶ καὶ θεοὶ αὐτοί the gods themselves may be turned  (by prayer), (Iliad by Homer); στρ. γλῶσσα a glib, pliant  tongue, (Iliad by Homer) 
5. bent, curved , of a pick-axe, (Euripides); of a bow, (Theocritus Poeta Bucolicus) (ML)
